targets.content: Mauritius aims to reduce overall GHG emissions by 40% in 2030 compared to the Business as Usual (BAU) scenario of around 6,900 ktCO2eq (including LULUCF) in 2030

The contribution by each sector to the 40% mitigation target in terms of avoided emissions (ktCO2eq) is as it follows:
Transport: 129 ktCO2eq
Mitigation efforts under the NDC 3.0 aim for a 40% reduction in economy-wide GHG emissions by 2035, relative to business-as-usual scenarios.
60% renewable energy in the electricity mix by 2035.
Coal phase out in electricity production by 2035.
Achieve 10% reduction in energy consumption through efficiency measures [by 2030]. Achieve 20% reduction in energy consumption through efficiency measures [by 2035].
Domestic aviation: Complete 70% electrification of Ground Support Equipment fleet [by 2030]. Complete 80% electrification of Ground Support Equipment fleet [by 2035].
Implement 5% SAF blend for domestic operations (conditional on supply chain development)
Achieve Net Zero for national airline ground operations
Achieve Net Zero on domestic operations
Replace at least 20% of the total fleet (electric) of 2000 buses by horizon 2035
National vision to decarbonise the public transport system by 2035.

Progressively green its light transit system by 2035 in 4 phases.
- Phase 1: Planned electrification of rooftop solar PV by early 2027 at a light rail depot and car 
parks for a total capacity of 1.3 MW, to displace about 15% of current electricity consumption.
This would require an estimated investment of USD 1.6 million (Rs 71.5 M) with 1.4 Kt CO2 to 
be avoided annually as from 2027.
- Phase 2: With the installation of 3 MW brown-field and additional PV by 2030 at an estimated 
cost of USD 3.6 M (Rs 165 million), the cumulative CO2 to be displaced is estimated at 4.7 Ktons.
- Phase 3: Installation of additional 2 MW of solar PV at an estimated cost USD 2.4 (Rs 110 M) 
will allow displacement of CO2 annually as from 2030.
- Phase 4: By 2035, 2.2 MW will be installed at a cost of USD 2.7 M (Rs 121 M) allowing a 
displacement of 9.45 Kt of CO2 annually
Resilient Infrastructure: Upgrade 100 km of roads and drains to flood-resilient standards by 2030, and 250 km by 2035.
Avoided emissions of 30 Gg CO2e by 2035, with respect to BAU emissions from 2016 levels.

measures.quote: promotion of energy efficient mass transportation systems based on hybrid technologies and cleaner energy sources

promotion of energy efficient mass transportation systems based on hybrid technologies and cleaner energy sources

Acquisition of hybrid and electric means of mass transportation
Extension of the light rail network as part of the national strategy to modernise and upscale the public transport system by 2022
phasing out of subsidies and incentives for the importation of diesel buses
increase of subsidy for the purchase of electric vehicles
Electric Vehicle Integration Road map 2020.
Divert 30% and 50% of on-board waste from landfill through circular economy principles by 2030 and 2035 repectively.

Alternative bunker fuels: The Port Master Plan provides for alternative bunker fuels to facilitate the transition to greener shipping practices … infrastructure capable of accommodating LNG, Ammonia, Biofuels, Methanol and other emerging low-carbon fuels.

measures.instrument: Support on-shore power and electric charging facilities in ports
measures.quote: Hybrid electric shore-side equipment: Mauritius aims to transition to hybrid and electric-powered shore-side cargo handling equipment, including Rubber-Tyred Gantries and tractor trailers.

Locally available alternate bunker fuels: Mauritius is working with the International Maritime Organisation to assess the potential use of locally available alternate bunker fuels for tugs and local shipping operations … to utilise renewable and waste-derived fuels to reduce reliance on fossil fuels, supporting circular economy principles.
The national aviation sector is fully committed to decarbonise its activities and has embarked on the Airport Carbon Accreditation programme which comprises 7 levels of certification.
Maintain 100% digital publications on-board instead of hard copies

Locally available alternate bunker fuels: Mauritius is working with the International Maritime Organisation to assess the potential use of locally available alternate bunker fuels for tugs and local shipping operations … to utilise renewable and waste-derived fuels to reduce reliance on fossil fuels, supporting circular economy principles.
20-year Master Plan … including walkability … to connect public, private transport and cycling infrastructure.
100 electric public buses introduced by Jan 2026 and another 100 lots subject to donor assistance.
Progressively green light transit system … Phases 1–4 installing solar PV capacity up to 8.5 MW by 2035, avoiding up to 9.45 kt CO₂ annually by 2035.
Progressively green light transit system … Phases 1–4 installing solar PV capacity up to 8.5 MW by 2035, avoiding up to 9.45 kt CO₂ annually by 2035.

The fleet of cars in Mauritius was increasing by 5% annually and taxation on conventional cars has recently been reviewed and a 2% reduction is foreseen in the new vehicle market. Accordingly, it is anticipated that this will cause an annual decrease of 14,000 vehicles, 22 million litres of fuel with a 52 ktons of CO2 emission reduction.
Adaptive Traffic Control System Policy by 2027 … optimise traffic flow, reduce congestion, fuel consumption and GHG emissions, resulting in reduction of around 25kt of CO2.

Reduce reliance on the grid through installation of rooftop solar PV on all bus depots. With this measure, annually, 9 million litres of diesel will be displaced with an estimated 24.1 kt of CO₂ reduction in addition to the contribution of the 400 e-buses by horizon 2035.

Reduce reliance on the grid through installation of rooftop solar PV on all bus depots. With this measure, annually, 9 million litres of diesel will be displaced with an estimated 24.1 kt of CO₂ reduction in addition to the contribution of the 400 e-buses by horizon 2035.

measures.instrument: General transport and land use planning
measures.quote: Mauritius will come up with a 20-year Master Plan … to provide a strategic orientation for the land transport sector. It will include walkability … to connect public, private transport and cycling infrastructure.

adaptation.quote: Protection of infrastructure will be enhanced against climate change calamities
Construction of a breakwater to enhance protection against sea-level rise, storm surges and extreme weather events, and to support expanded port capacity.
Aviation sector is also embarking on the development of Climate Change Mitigation and Adaptation Plans.
Roads, drains and flood-control works designed and upgraded to international flood-resilient standards (AASHTO/DMRB/TRL)
